sub_mechanisms: M_1: define reasoning-problem 'Galois group' over CoT step-extension field
M_2: solvability test via composition-series with abelian quotients
M_3: solvable problems get linear-CoT radical-tower decomposition
M_4: non-solvable problems flagged for tree/graph search
stripped_llm_application: For each reasoning problem, hypothesize a group-theoretic invariant from the problem structure; test if the invariant is in a 'solvable' class (chain of abelian quotients); if yes, decompose reasoning into linear chain of radical-like steps; if no, flag for tree/graph search instead.
novelty_claim_stripped: Hi-CoT and LCoT2Tree analyze CoT structure empirically; this candidate uses GROUP-THEORETIC solvability as a theoretical bound on which problems admit linear CoT.
